title: Deliciously Warm Canadian Lentil Curry
description: A hearty and nutritious Canadian dish featuring red lentils, warm water, and a blend of ayurvedic spices, cooked to perfection in a tandoor-inspired method. This recipe is ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly and perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the rinsed lentils and water, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
In a large skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of oil over medium heat, and saute the chopped onion until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet, and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the curry powder, turmeric, cumin, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the cooked lentil mixture to the skillet, and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Reduce the heat to low, and simmer the curry for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors have melded together and the curry has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, then serve the curry hot over rice or with naan bread.
